sentence_reviews.text: External or constructed signals specify desired predictions, actions, rankings, or behavior.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: supervised learning, classification/regression, ranking losses, active learning, semi/weakly supervised learning, and reinforcement learning (lines 3-14, 515-519, and 588-606); AIMA contents.html: supervised learning, decision/reward material, and reinforcement learning from rewards (lines 495-508 and 599-615).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support labels/examples, losses, ranking, rewards, and preferences/decision signals as learning signals. The exact compact definition is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Game theory & decision theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Labels, targets, rewards & preferences rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Game theory & decision theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 5 'Decision Theory' and active learning decision-theoretic approach (lines 148-178 and 596-599); AIMA contents.html: game theory, sequential decisions, MDPs, and rewards (lines 435-480 and 599-615).
sentence_reviews.notes: Decision and reward material supports the dependency; game theory is more relevant to preferences and multiagent settings than to all supervision signals.
